questions: Why does MDE matter in experiment design?
qa.answer: MDE is the smallest true effect size your test is powered to detect at chosen confidence. Lower MDE requires larger samples or longer duration; too‑high MDE risks missing meaningful lifts.
implication: Set MDE based on business impact (e.g., minimum lift worth shipping).
Use power calculators to size samples/duration before launching.
Avoid peeking; commit to stop rules to protect Type I/II error.
